summary: Chinese-French Nobel laureate (2000). Known for the play Bus Stop and the novel Soul Mountain. Exiled from China; settled in France. Bus Stop was banned in China for its Beckett-like qualities.

works.name: Bus Stop
works.description: Play about characters waiting at a bus stop, with Beckett-like absurdist qualities.

clues.clue: Characters like Hothead, Glasses, and the Carpenter discuss why they're waiting at the title bus stop; includes 'polyphonic dialogue'
examples: characters like Hothead, Glasses, and the Carpenter discuss why they're waiting at the title location

clues.clue: Card Player rigs a game; Man discovers but Crowd tries to convince him he's wrong
examples: the Man discovers that the Card Player has rigged the game, but the Crowd tries to convince the Man that he is wrong

works.name: Soul Mountain
works.description: Novel written during a journey through remote China.

clues.clue: Written during a journey through remote southwestern China; alternates between 'I' and 'you' narratives
examples: written during a journey through remote China

clues.clue: Chinese author exiled to France; won 2000 Nobel Prize in Literature
examples: a writer from one of these two countries was exiled to the other

comprehensive_summary: Gao Xingjian (born 1940) is a Chinese-French author who won the 2000 Nobel Prize in Literature. His play Bus Stop features characters like Hothead, Glasses, and the Carpenter discussing why they're waiting at a bus stop, using 'polyphonic dialogue.' It was banned in China. Soul Mountain was written during a journey through remote southwestern China, alternating 'I' and 'you' narratives.

cards.front: Play: characters Hothead, Glasses, and the Carpenter discuss why they're waiting at the title location
Novel: written during a journey through remote southwestern China
